outcome: zBMI (Medium Term) - Subgroup Analysis - Socioeconomic Status
source_quote: No subgroup analysis by socioeconomic status for BMI z-score reported in the paper. Food security subgroup mentioned: food secure Wellness Journey children had a loss of -0.07 BMI z-score, food insecure Wellness Journey children had a gain of 0.12 BMI z-score (nonsignificant).
Child BMI (kg/m2): Mailed baseline 17.5 (SD 2.5), post 18.0 (SD 3.2); Mentored baseline 17.3 (SD 1.6), post 17.9 (SD 2.5); P = 0.701 for mentor effect
Child BMI percentile: Mailed baseline 75.5 (SD 23.0), post 79.0 (SD 20.0); Mentored baseline 78.8 (SD 20.7), post 82.0 (SD 18.8); P = 0.913 for mentor effect
